```{r}
# Reorder levels so that "Drowsy" (the positive class) is first.
train$label <- factor(train$label, levels = c("Drowsy", "Natural"))
test$label  <- factor(test$label,  levels = c("Drowsy", "Natural"))

# Set up parallel processing
num_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1  
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoParallel(cl)

# Define cross-validation method for training.
# We add classProbs = TRUE and summaryFunction = defaultSummary to obtain ROC and probability estimates.
train_control <- trainControl(
  method = "cv",
  number = 5,
  allowParallel = TRUE,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = defaultSummary
)

# Train a Random Forest model using caret's "rf" method
rf_model <- train(
  label ~ ., 
  data = train, 
  method = "rf", 
  trControl = train_control,
  preProcess = NULL,
  metric = "Accuracy"  # Optimize model using ROC
)

# Stop the parallel cluster after training
stopCluster(cl)

# Print the results and best tuning parameters
print(rf_model$results)
print(rf_model$bestTune)

###########################################
# 1. Save the model and show how to call it later
###########################################
saveRDS(rf_model, file = "Base_Models/RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering.rds")
cat("Model saved as 'Base_Models/RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering.rds'. To load it later, use:\n")
cat("loaded_model <- readRDS('Base_Models/RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering.rds')\n\n")

###########################################
# 2. Save bestTune hyperparameters as CSV
#    (include a column 'model' with the label "RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering")
###########################################
best_tune_df <- cbind(model = "RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering", rf_model$bestTune)
write.csv(best_tune_df, "Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/BestHyperparams_RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("BestTune hyperparameters saved as 'Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/BestHyperparams_RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n\n")

###########################################
# 3. Make predictions and create confusion matrix CSV with TP, FP, FN, TN
###########################################
# Predict on the test set (for class labels)
predictions <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test)

# Compute the confusion matrix
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(predictions, test$label)
print(conf_matrix)

# Extract confusion matrix values; assumes binary classification.
levels_labels <- levels(test$label)
if(length(levels_labels) == 2) {
  positive_class <- levels_labels[1]  # "Drowsy"
  negative_class <- levels_labels[2]  # "Natural"
  
  cm_table <- conf_matrix$table
  TP <- cm_table[positive_class, positive_class]
  FP <- cm_table[positive_class, negative_class]
  FN <- cm_table[negative_class, positive_class]
  TN <- cm_table[negative_class, negative_class]
  
  cm_df <- data.frame(model = "RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering",
                      TP = TP,
                      FP = FP,
                      FN = FN,
                      TN = TN)
  
  write.csv(cm_df, "Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ConfusionMatrix_RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Confusion matrix saved as 'Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ConfusionMatrix_RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 4. Calculate and save F1-score, Precision, Recall, Accuracy, and AUC as CSV
  ###########################################
  accuracy  <- conf_matrix$overall["Accuracy"]
  recall    <- conf_matrix$byClass["Sensitivity"]
  precision <- conf_matrix$byClass["Pos Pred Value"]
  f1_score  <- 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)
  
  # Obtain probability predictions for ROC curve (type = "prob")
  probs <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test, type = "prob")
  # Use probability for the positive class ("Drowsy")
  roc_obj <- roc(response = test$label, predictor = probs[[positive_class]])
  auc_val <- as.numeric(auc(roc_obj))
  
  metrics_df <- data.frame(model = "RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering",
                           Accuracy = accuracy,
                           Precision = precision,
                           Recall = recall,
                           FOne = f1_score,
                           AUC = auc_val)
  
  write.csv(metrics_df, "Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/Metrics_RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Performance metrics saved as 'Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/Metrics_RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 5. Create and save an AUC ROC plot as a PNG
  ###########################################
  # Build ROC data: fpr (1 - specificity) and tpr (sensitivity)
  roc_data <- data.frame(fpr = 1 - roc_obj$specificities, tpr = roc_obj$sensitivities)
  
  write.csv(roc_data, "Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ROCData_RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("ROC data saved as 'Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ROCData_RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n")
  
  # Generate the ROC plot using ggplot2
  roc_plot <- ggplot(roc_data, aes(x = fpr, y = tpr)) +
    geom_line(color = "blue") +  # ROC curve in blue
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +  # 45° dotted reference line
    labs(x = "False Positive Rate (1 - Specificity)",
         y = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)",
         title = "ROC Curve for RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering") +
    theme_minimal() +
    # Annotate AUC in the top right corner (rounded to 3 decimals)
    annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = 0.95, 
             label = paste("AUC =", format(round(auc_val, 3), nsmall = 3)), 
             color = "red", size = 5)
  
  # Save the ROC plot as a PNG file
  ggsave("Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ROC_RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering.png", 
         plot = roc_plot, width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)
  cat("ROC plot saved as 'Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ROC_RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering.png'.\n")
  
} else {
  cat("Skipping confusion matrix extraction and ROC analysis: classification is not binary.\n")
}

```

Gaussian SVM, feature pre-processing

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(caret)
library(ggcorrplot)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(kableExtra)
library(knitr)
library(purrr)
library(kernlab)
library(BiocManager)
library(readr)
library(doParallel)
library(EBImage)
# Added for ROC analysis
library(pROC)

set.seed(100)

# Load training and testing data
train <- read_csv("Prepped_Data/trainWithFeatureEngineering.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
test <- read_csv("Prepped_Data/testWithFeatureEngineeringAblation.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Ensure that the target variable is a factor.
# If necessary, reorder levels so that "Drowsy" is the positive class.
train$label <- factor(train$label, levels = c("Drowsy", "Natural"))
test$label  <- factor(test$label,  levels = c("Drowsy", "Natural"))

# Set up parallel processing
num_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1  
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoParallel(cl)

# Define cross-validation method for training.
# Note: We add classProbs = TRUE and summaryFunction for ROC
train_control <- trainControl(
  method = "cv",
  number = 5,
  allowParallel = TRUE,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = defaultSummary
)

# Train an SVM model.
# The additional argument prob.model = TRUE ensures that ksvm computes probabilities.
svm_model <- train(
  label ~ ., 
  data = train, 
  method = "svmRadial", 
  trControl = train_control,
  preProcess = NULL,
  metric = "Accuracy",   # Optimize model using ROC
  prob.model = TRUE # Ensure probability estimates are computed
)

# Stop the parallel cluster after training
stopCluster(cl)

# Display model results
print(svm_model$results)
print(svm_model$bestTune)

###########################################
# 1. Save the model and show how to call it later
###########################################
saveRDS(svm_model, file = "Base_Models/SVMModelWithFeatureEngineering.rds")
cat("Model saved as 'Base_Models/SVMModelWithFeatureEngineering.rds'. To load it later, use:\n")
cat("loaded_model <- readRDS('Base_Models/SVMModelWithFeatureEngineering.rds')\n\n")

###########################################
# 2. Save bestTune hyperparameters as CSV
#    (include a column 'model' with the label "SVMWithFeatureEngineering")
###########################################
best_tune_df <- cbind(model = "SVMWithFeatureEngineering", svm_model$bestTune)
write.csv(best_tune_df, "Base_Models_Data/SVM/BestHyperparams_SVMWithFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("BestTune hyperparameters saved as 'Base_Models_Data/SVM/BestHyperparams_SVMWithFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n\n")

###########################################
# 3. Make predictions and create confusion matrix CSV with TP, FP, FN, TN
###########################################
# Predict on the test set (for class labels)
predictions <- predict(svm_model, newdata = test)

# Compute the confusion matrix
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(predictions, test$label)
print(conf_matrix)

# Extract confusion matrix table; assumes binary classification.
# Here we define the first level as the positive class ("Drowsy").
levels_labels <- levels(test$label)
if(length(levels_labels) == 2) {
  positive_class <- levels_labels[1]  # "Drowsy"
  negative_class <- levels_labels[2]  # "Natural"
  
  cm_table <- conf_matrix$table
  TP <- cm_table[positive_class, positive_class]
  FP <- cm_table[positive_class, negative_class]
  FN <- cm_table[negative_class, positive_class]
  TN <- cm_table[negative_class, negative_class]
  
  cm_df <- data.frame(model = "SVMWithFeatureEngineering",
                      TP = TP,
                      FP = FP,
                      FN = FN,
                      TN = TN)
  
  write.csv(cm_df, "Base_Models_Data/SVM/ConfusionMatrix_SVMWithFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Confusion matrix saved as 'Base_Models_Data/SVM/ConfusionMatrix_SVMWithFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 4. Calculate and save F1-score, Precision, Recall, Accuracy, and AUC as CSV
  ###########################################
  accuracy <- conf_matrix$overall["Accuracy"]
  recall <- conf_matrix$byClass["Sensitivity"]
  precision <- conf_matrix$byClass["Pos Pred Value"]
  f1_score <- 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)
  
  # Obtain probability predictions for ROC curve (now that we enabled probabilities)
  probs <- predict(svm_model, newdata = test, type = "prob")
  # Use probability for the positive class ("Drowsy")
  roc_obj <- roc(response = test$label, predictor = probs[[positive_class]])
  auc_val <- as.numeric(auc(roc_obj))
  
  metrics_df <- data.frame(model = "SVMWithFeatureEngineering",
                           Accuracy = accuracy,
                           Precision = precision,
                           Recall = recall,
                           FOne = f1_score,
                           AUC = auc_val)
  
  write.csv(metrics_df, "Base_Models_Data/SVM/Metrics_SVMWithFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Performance metrics saved as 'Base_Models_Data/SVM/Metrics_SVMWithFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 5. Create and save an AUC ROC plot as a PNG
  ###########################################
  # Build data for ROC curve: fpr (1 - specificity) and tpr (sensitivity)
  roc_data <- data.frame(fpr = 1 - roc_obj$specificities, tpr = roc_obj$sensitivities)
  
  write.csv(roc_data, "Base_Models_Data/SVM/ROCData_SVMWithFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("ROC Data for plot building saved as 'Base_Models_Data/SVM/ROCData_SVMWithFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n")
  
  # Generate the ROC plot using ggplot2.
  roc_plot <- ggplot(roc_data, aes(x = fpr, y = tpr)) +
    geom_line(color = "blue") +  # ROC curve in blue
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +  # Dotted 45° reference line
    labs(x = "False Positive Rate (1 - Specificity)",
         y = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)",
         title = "ROC Curve for SVMWithFeatureEngineering") +
    theme_minimal() +
    # Annotate AUC in the top right corner, rounded to 3 significant figures.
    annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = 0.95, 
             label = paste("AUC =", format(round(auc_val, 3), nsmall = 3)), 
             color = "red", size = 5)
  
  # Save the ROC plot as a PNG file.
  ggsave("Base_Models_Data/SVM/ROC_SVMWithFeatureEngineering.png", 
         plot = roc_plot, width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)
  cat("ROC plot saved as 'Base_Models_Data/SVM/ROC_SVMWithFeatureEngineering.png'.\n")
  
} else {
  cat("Skipping confusion matrix extraction and ROC analysis: classification is not binary.\n")
}

```

XGBoost, feature pre-processing

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(caret)
library(ggcorrplot)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(kableExtra)
library(knitr)
library(purrr)
library(kernlab)
library(BiocManager)
library(readr)
library(doParallel)
library(EBImage)
library(xgboost)  # For the xgbTree method
# Added for ROC analysis
library(pROC)

set.seed(100)

# Load training and testing data
train <- read_csv("Prepped_Data/trainWithFeatureEngineeringPCA.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
test <- read_csv("Prepped_Data/testWithFeatureEngineeringAblationPCA.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Ensure that the target variable is a factor.
# Reorder levels so that "Drowsy" is the positive class.
train$label <- factor(train$label, levels = c("Drowsy", "Natural"))
test$label  <- factor(test$label,  levels = c("Drowsy", "Natural"))

# Set up parallel processing
num_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1  
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoParallel(cl)

# Define cross-validation method for training.
# We add classProbs = TRUE and summaryFunction = defaultSummary to get AUC and probability estimates.
train_control <- trainControl(
  method = "cv",
  number = 5,
  allowParallel = TRUE,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = defaultSummary
)

# Train an XGBoost model using caret's xgbTree method
xgb_model <- train(
  label ~ ., 
  data = train, 
  method = "xgbTree", 
  trControl = train_control,
  preProcess = NULL,
  metric = "Accuracy"  # Optimize model using ROC
)

# Stop the parallel cluster after training
stopCluster(cl)

# Print the results and best tuning parameters
print(xgb_model$results)
print(xgb_model$bestTune)

###########################################
# 1. Save the model and show how to call it later
###########################################
saveRDS(xgb_model, file = "Base_Models/XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering.rds")
cat("Model saved as 'Base_Models/XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering.rds'. To load it later, use:\n")
cat("loaded_model <- readRDS('Base_Models/XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering.rds')\n\n")

###########################################
# 2. Save bestTune hyperparameters as CSV
#    (include a column 'model' with the label "XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering")
###########################################
best_tune_df <- cbind(model = "XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering", xgb_model$bestTune)
write.csv(best_tune_df, "Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/BestHyperparams_XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("BestTune hyperparameters saved as 'Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/BestHyperparams_XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n\n")

###########################################
# 3. Make predictions and create confusion matrix CSV with TP, FP, FN, TN
###########################################
# Predict on the test set (for class labels)
predictions <- predict(xgb_model, newdata = test)

# Compute the confusion matrix
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(predictions, test$label)
print(conf_matrix)

# Extract confusion matrix table; assumes binary classification.
# Here we define the first level as the positive class ("Drowsy").
levels_labels <- levels(test$label)
if(length(levels_labels) == 2) {
  positive_class <- levels_labels[1]  # "Drowsy"
  negative_class <- levels_labels[2]  # "Natural"
  
  cm_table <- conf_matrix$table
  TP <- cm_table[positive_class, positive_class]
  FP <- cm_table[positive_class, negative_class]
  FN <- cm_table[negative_class, positive_class]
  TN <- cm_table[negative_class, negative_class]
  
  cm_df <- data.frame(model = "XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering",
                      TP = TP,
                      FP = FP,
                      FN = FN,
                      TN = TN)
  
  write.csv(cm_df, "Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/ConfusionMatrix_XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Confusion matrix saved as 'Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/ConfusionMatrix_XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 4. Calculate and save F1-score, Precision, Recall, Accuracy, and AUC as CSV
  ###########################################
  accuracy <- conf_matrix$overall["Accuracy"]
  recall <- conf_matrix$byClass["Sensitivity"]
  precision <- conf_matrix$byClass["Pos Pred Value"]
  f1_score <- 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)
  
  # Obtain probability predictions for ROC curve (ensure type = "prob")
  probs <- predict(xgb_model, newdata = test, type = "prob")
  # Use probability for the positive class ("Drowsy")
  roc_obj <- roc(response = test$label, predictor = probs[[positive_class]])
  auc_val <- as.numeric(auc(roc_obj))
  
  metrics_df <- data.frame(model = "XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering",
                           Accuracy = accuracy,
                           Precision = precision,
                           Recall = recall,
                           FOne = f1_score,
                           AUC = auc_val)
  
  write.csv(metrics_df, "Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/Metrics_XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Performance metrics saved as 'Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/Metrics_XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 5. Create and save an AUC ROC plot as a PNG
  ###########################################
  # Build data for ROC curve: fpr (1 - specificity) and tpr (sensitivity)
  roc_data <- data.frame(fpr = 1 - roc_obj$specificities, tpr = roc_obj$sensitivities)
  
  write.csv(roc_data, "Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/ROCData_XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("ROC Data for plot building saved as 'Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/ROCData_XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n")
  
  # Generate the ROC plot using ggplot2.
  roc_plot <- ggplot(roc_data, aes(x = fpr, y = tpr)) +
    geom_line(color = "blue") +  # ROC curve in blue
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +  # Dotted 45° reference line
    labs(x = "False Positive Rate (1 - Specificity)",
         y = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)",
         title = "ROC Curve for XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering") +
    theme_minimal() +
    # Annotate AUC in the top right corner, rounded to 3 significant figures.
    annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = 0.95, 
             label = paste("AUC =", format(round(auc_val, 3), nsmall = 3)), 
             color = "red", size = 5)
  
  # Save the ROC plot as a PNG file.
  ggsave("Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/ROC_XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering.png", 
         plot = roc_plot, width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)
  cat("ROC plot saved as 'Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/ROC_XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering.png'.\n")
  
} else {
  cat("Skipping confusion matrix extraction and ROC analysis: classification is not binary.\n")
}

```

Random Forest, feature pre-processing


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(caret)
library(ggcorrplot)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(kableExtra)
library(knitr)
library(purrr)
library(kernlab)
library(BiocManager)
library(readr)
library(doParallel)
library(EBImage)
# Random Forest can be run using caret, so an explicit load of randomForest is optional.
# library(randomForest)
# Added for ROC analysis
library(pROC)

set.seed(100)

# Load training and testing data
train <- read_csv("Prepped_Data/trainWithFeatureEngineering.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
test  <- read_csv("Prepped_Data/testWithFeatureEngineeringAblation.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# Ensure that the target variable is a factor.
# Reorder levels so that "Drowsy" (the positive class) is first.
train$label <- factor(train$label, levels = c("Drowsy", "Natural"))
test$label  <- factor(test$label,  levels = c("Drowsy", "Natural"))

# Set up parallel processing
num_cores <- parallel::detectCores() - 1  
cl <- makeCluster(num_cores)
registerDoParallel(cl)

# Define cross-validation method for training.
# We add classProbs = TRUE and summaryFunction = defaultSummary to obtain ROC and probability estimates.
train_control <- trainControl(
  method = "cv",
  number = 5,
  allowParallel = TRUE,
  classProbs = TRUE,
  summaryFunction = defaultSummary
)

# Train a Random Forest model using caret's "rf" method
rf_model <- train(
  label ~ ., 
  data = train, 
  method = "rf", 
  trControl = train_control,
  preProcess = NULL,
  metric = "Accuracy"  # Optimize model using ROC
)

# Stop the parallel cluster after training
stopCluster(cl)

# Print the results and best tuning parameters
print(rf_model$results)
print(rf_model$bestTune)

###########################################
# 1. Save the model and show how to call it later
###########################################
saveRDS(rf_model, file = "Base_Models/RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering.rds")
cat("Model saved as 'Base_Models/RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering.rds'. To load it later, use:\n")
cat("loaded_model <- readRDS('Base_Models/RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering.rds')\n\n")

###########################################
# 2. Save bestTune hyperparameters as CSV
#    (include a column 'model' with the label "RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering")
###########################################
best_tune_df <- cbind(model = "RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering", rf_model$bestTune)
write.csv(best_tune_df, "Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/BestHyperparams_RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("BestTune hyperparameters saved as 'Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/BestHyperparams_RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n\n")

###########################################
# 3. Make predictions and create confusion matrix CSV with TP, FP, FN, TN
###########################################
# Predict on the test set (for class labels)
predictions <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test)

# Compute the confusion matrix
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(predictions, test$label)
print(conf_matrix)

# Extract confusion matrix values; assumes binary classification.
levels_labels <- levels(test$label)
if(length(levels_labels) == 2) {
  positive_class <- levels_labels[1]  # "Drowsy"
  negative_class <- levels_labels[2]  # "Natural"
  
  cm_table <- conf_matrix$table
  TP <- cm_table[positive_class, positive_class]
  FP <- cm_table[positive_class, negative_class]
  FN <- cm_table[negative_class, positive_class]
  TN <- cm_table[negative_class, negative_class]
  
  cm_df <- data.frame(model = "RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering",
                      TP = TP,
                      FP = FP,
                      FN = FN,
                      TN = TN)
  
  write.csv(cm_df, "Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ConfusionMatrix_RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Confusion matrix saved as 'Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ConfusionMatrix_RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 4. Calculate and save F1-score, Precision, Recall, Accuracy, and AUC as CSV
  ###########################################
  accuracy  <- conf_matrix$overall["Accuracy"]
  recall    <- conf_matrix$byClass["Sensitivity"]
  precision <- conf_matrix$byClass["Pos Pred Value"]
  f1_score  <- 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)
  
  # Obtain probability predictions for ROC curve (type = "prob")
  probs <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test, type = "prob")
  # Use probability for the positive class ("Drowsy")
  roc_obj <- roc(response = test$label, predictor = probs[[positive_class]])
  auc_val <- as.numeric(auc(roc_obj))
  
  metrics_df <- data.frame(model = "RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering",
                           Accuracy = accuracy,
                           Precision = precision,
                           Recall = recall,
                           FOne = f1_score,
                           AUC = auc_val)
  
  write.csv(metrics_df, "Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/Metrics_RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Performance metrics saved as 'Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/Metrics_RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 5. Create and save an AUC ROC plot as a PNG
  ###########################################
  # Build ROC data: fpr (1 - specificity) and tpr (sensitivity)
  roc_data <- data.frame(fpr = 1 - roc_obj$specificities, tpr = roc_obj$sensitivities)
  
  write.csv(roc_data, "Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ROCData_RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("ROC data saved as 'Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ROCData_RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering.csv'.\n")
  
  # Generate the ROC plot using ggplot2
  roc_plot <- ggplot(roc_data, aes(x = fpr, y = tpr)) +
    geom_line(color = "blue") +  # ROC curve in blue
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +  # 45° dotted reference line
    labs(x = "False Positive Rate (1 - Specificity)",
         y = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)",
         title = "ROC Curve for RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering") +
    theme_minimal() +
    # Annotate AUC in the top right corner (rounded to 3 decimals)
    annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = 0.95, 
             label = paste("AUC =", format(round(auc_val, 3), nsmall = 3)), 
             color = "red", size = 5)
  
  # Save the ROC plot as a PNG file
  ggsave("Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ROC_RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering.png", 
         plot = roc_plot, width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)
  cat("ROC plot saved as 'Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ROC_RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering.png'.\n")
  
} else {
  cat("Skipping confusion matrix extraction and ROC analysis: classification is not binary.\n")
}

```

### Data Metrics Combining
SVM classification report construction and best hyperparameter csv construction


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Define the base path
base_path <- "Base_Models_Data/SVM/"

# List files, excluding ROCData and ROC files (only reading Metrics, ConfusionMatrix, and BestHyperparams)
metrics_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^Metrics_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
confusion_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^ConfusionMatrix_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
hyperparam_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^BestHyperparams_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

# Function to extract model label from filename given a specific prefix.
# For example, for filename "Metrics_SVMNoFeatureEngineeringPCA.csv" with prefix "Metrics_",
# the function will return "SVMNoFeatureEngineeringPCA".
extract_model_label <- function(filename, prefix) {
  label <- basename(filename)
  label <- sub(paste0("^", prefix), "", label)
  label <- sub("\\.csv$", "", label)
  return(label)
}

# Create a named vector of confusion files keyed by their extracted model label.
confusion_map <- setNames(confusion_files, sapply(confusion_files, extract_model_label, prefix = "ConfusionMatrix_"))

# Initialize a list to store the joined classification data frames
classification_list <- list()

# Loop over each Metrics file and find the corresponding ConfusionMatrix file by an exact filename match.
for (metrics_file in metrics_files) {
  # Extract the model label from the Metrics file name.
  m_model <- extract_model_label(metrics_file, prefix = "Metrics_")
  
  # Look up the corresponding ConfusionMatrix file using the full model label.
  if (m_model %in% names(confusion_map)) {
    conf_file <- confusion_map[[m_model]]
    
    # Read the Metrics CSV and ConfusionMatrix CSV. They already include a "model" column.
    metrics_df <- read_csv(metrics_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
    confusion_df <- read_csv(conf_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
    
    # Join the two data frames by the "model" column.
    joined_df <- inner_join(metrics_df, confusion_df, by = "model")
    
    # Store the joined data frame in a list.
    classification_list[[m_model]] <- joined_df
  } else {
    warning("No matching ConfusionMatrix file found for model '", m_model, "'.")
  }
}

# Rbind (stack) all joined classification reports into one data frame
combined_classification <- bind_rows(classification_list)

# Read and rbind all BestHyperparams CSV files
hyperparams_list <- lapply(hyperparam_files, function(file) {
  read_csv(file, show_col_types = FALSE)
})
combined_hyperparameters <- bind_rows(hyperparams_list)

# Write the combined data frames to CSV files
write_csv(combined_classification, file.path(base_path, "SVMCombinedClassificationReport.csv"))
write_csv(combined_hyperparameters, file.path(base_path, "SVMHyperparameters.csv"))


```
XGBoost classification report construction and best hyperparameter csv construction


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Define the base path for XGBoost
base_path <- "Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/"

# List files, excluding ROCData and ROC files (only reading Metrics, ConfusionMatrix, and BestHyperparams)
metrics_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^Metrics_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
confusion_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^ConfusionMatrix_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
hyperparam_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^BestHyperparams_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

# Function to extract model label from filename given a specific prefix.
# For example, for filename "Metrics_XGBoostNoFeatureEngineeringPCA.csv" with prefix "Metrics_",
# the function will return "XGBoostNoFeatureEngineeringPCA".
extract_model_label <- function(filename, prefix) {
  label <- basename(filename)
  label <- sub(paste0("^", prefix), "", label)
  label <- sub("\\.csv$", "", label)
  return(label)
}

# Create a named vector of confusion files keyed by their extracted model label.
confusion_map <- setNames(confusion_files, sapply(confusion_files, extract_model_label, prefix = "ConfusionMatrix_"))

# Initialize a list to store the joined classification data frames
classification_list <- list()

# Loop over each Metrics file and find the corresponding ConfusionMatrix file by an exact filename match.
for (metrics_file in metrics_files) {
  # Extract the model label from the Metrics file name.
  m_model <- extract_model_label(metrics_file, prefix = "Metrics_")
  
  # Look up the corresponding ConfusionMatrix file using the full model label.
  if (m_model %in% names(confusion_map)) {
    conf_file <- confusion_map[[m_model]]
    
    # Read the Metrics CSV and ConfusionMatrix CSV. They already include a "model" column.
    metrics_df <- read_csv(metrics_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
    confusion_df <- read_csv(conf_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
    
    # Join the two data frames by the "model" column.
    joined_df <- inner_join(metrics_df, confusion_df, by = "model")
    
    # Store the joined data frame in a list.
    classification_list[[m_model]] <- joined_df
  } else {
    warning("No matching ConfusionMatrix file found for model '", m_model, "'.")
  }
}

# Rbind (stack) all joined classification reports into one data frame
combined_classification <- bind_rows(classification_list)

# Read and rbind all BestHyperparams CSV files
hyperparams_list <- lapply(hyperparam_files, function(file) {
  read_csv(file, show_col_types = FALSE)
})
combined_hyperparameters <- bind_rows(hyperparams_list)

# Write the combined data frames to CSV files
write_csv(combined_classification, file.path(base_path, "XGBoostCombinedClassificationReport.csv"))
write_csv(combined_hyperparameters, file.path(base_path, "XGBoostHyperparameters.csv"))

```
RandomForest classification report construction and best hyperparameter csv construction



```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Define the base path for RandomForest
base_path <- "Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/"

# List files, excluding ROCData and ROC files (only reading Metrics, ConfusionMatrix, and BestHyperparams)
metrics_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^Metrics_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
confusion_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^ConfusionMatrix_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
hyperparam_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^BestHyperparams_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

# Function to extract model label from filename given a specific prefix.
# For example, for filename "Metrics_RandomForestNoFeatureEngineeringPCA.csv" with prefix "Metrics_",
# the function will return "RandomForestNoFeatureEngineeringPCA".
extract_model_label <- function(filename, prefix) {
  label <- basename(filename)
  label <- sub(paste0("^", prefix), "", label)
  label <- sub("\\.csv$", "", label)
  return(label)
}

# Create a named vector of confusion files keyed by their extracted model label.
confusion_map <- setNames(confusion_files, sapply(confusion_files, extract_model_label, prefix = "ConfusionMatrix_"))

# Initialize a list to store the joined classification data frames
classification_list <- list()

# Loop over each Metrics file and find the corresponding ConfusionMatrix file by an exact filename match.
for (metrics_file in metrics_files) {
  # Extract the model label from the Metrics file name.
  m_model <- extract_model_label(metrics_file, prefix = "Metrics_")
  
  # Look up the corresponding ConfusionMatrix file using the full model label.
  if (m_model %in% names(confusion_map)) {
    conf_file <- confusion_map[[m_model]]
    
    # Read the Metrics CSV and ConfusionMatrix CSV. They already include a "model" column.
    metrics_df <- read_csv(metrics_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
    confusion_df <- read_csv(conf_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
    
    # Join the two data frames by the "model" column.
    joined_df <- inner_join(metrics_df, confusion_df, by = "model")
    
    # Store the joined data frame in a list.
    classification_list[[m_model]] <- joined_df
  } else {
    warning("No matching ConfusionMatrix file found for model '", m_model, "'.")
  }
}

# Rbind (stack) all joined classification reports into one data frame
combined_classification <- bind_rows(classification_list)

# Read and rbind all BestHyperparams CSV files
hyperparams_list <- lapply(hyperparam_files, function(file) {
  read_csv(file, show_col_types = FALSE)
})
combined_hyperparameters <- bind_rows(hyperparams_list)

# Write the combined data frames to CSV files
write_csv(combined_classification, file.path(base_path, "RandomForestCombinedClassificationReport.csv"))
write_csv(combined_hyperparameters, file.path(base_path, "RandomForestHyperparameters.csv"))

```

Combine all to make the final base model report:
```{R}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Define file paths for each combined classification report
rf_report <- "Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/RandomForestCombinedClassificationReport.csv"
xgb_report <- "Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/XGBoostCombinedClassificationReport.csv"
svm_report <- "Base_Models_Data/SVM/SVMCombinedClassificationReport.csv"

# Read each CSV into a data frame
rf_df <- read_csv(rf_report, show_col_types = FALSE)
xgb_df <- read_csv(xgb_report, show_col_types = FALSE)
svm_df <- read_csv(svm_report, show_col_types = FALSE)

# Rbind (stack) the three data frames into one and then sort by accuracy then ROC (both in descending order)
final_report <- bind_rows(rf_df, xgb_df, svm_df) %>%
  arrange(desc(Accuracy), desc(AUC))

# Define the output path and write the final combined data frame as CSV
output_path <- "Base_Models_Data/FinalBaseModelReport.csv"
write_csv(final_report, output_path)


```

Combine Hyperparameters to a final report
```{R}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Define file paths for each combined classification report
rf_report <- "Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/RandomForestHyperparameters.csv"
xgb_report <- "Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/XGBoostHyperparameters.csv"
svm_report <- "Base_Models_Data/SVM/SVMHyperparameters.csv"

# Read each CSV into a data frame
rf_df <- read_csv(rf_report, show_col_types = FALSE)
xgb_df <- read_csv(xgb_report, show_col_types = FALSE)
svm_df <- read_csv(svm_report, show_col_types = FALSE)


# Define the output path and write the final combined data frame as CSV
write_csv(rf_df, "Base_Models_Data/RandomForestHyperparameters.csv")
write_csv(xgb_df, "Base_Models_Data/XGBoostHyperparameters.csv")
write_csv(svm_df, "Base_Models_Data/SVMHyperparameters.csv")
```
### AUCROC Plot Construction
SVM Plot

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1) Trapezoidal rule for AUC
calc_auc <- function(x, y) {
  sum(diff(x) * (head(y, -1) + tail(y, -1)) / 2)
}

# 2) Define ROCData file paths (in any order) and their descriptive labels
roc_files <- c(
  "Base_Models_Data/SVM/ROCData_SVMNoFeatureEngineering.csv",
  "Base_Models_Data/SVM/ROCData_SVMNoFeatureEngineeringPCA.csv",
  "Base_Models_Data/SVM/ROCData_SVMWithFeatureEngineering.csv",
  "Base_Models_Data/SVM/ROCData_SVMWithFeatureEngineeringPCA.csv"
)

# We'll map the files to labels initially (the final legend order will follow AUC)
curve_labels <- c(
  "No pre-processing + No feature engineering",
  "No pre-processing + Feature engineering",
  "Pre-processing + No feature engineering",
  "Pre-processing + Feature engineering"
)

# 3) Read, compute AUC, store results
roc_list <- list()
auc_values <- numeric(length(roc_files))

for(i in seq_along(roc_files)) {
  df <- read_csv(roc_files[i], show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    arrange(fpr)  # ensure ascending fpr
  
  this_auc <- calc_auc(df$fpr, df$tpr)
  auc_values[i] <- this_auc
  
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(AUC = this_auc,
           Curve = curve_labels[i])
  
  roc_list[[i]] <- df
}

# 4) Combine all ROC data
roc_data <- bind_rows(roc_list)

# 5) Determine factor order by descending AUC
auc_summary <- roc_data %>%
  group_by(Curve) %>%
  summarize(AUC = unique(AUC), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(AUC))

# Convert "Curve" to a factor with levels in descending AUC order
roc_data$Curve <- factor(roc_data$Curve, levels = auc_summary$Curve, ordered = TRUE)

# 6) Define a custom color vector to match the descending AUC order.
#    Highest AUC -> green, second -> blue, third -> purple, fourth -> red
color_vec <- c("green", "blue", "purple", "red")

# 7) Build annotation data for bottom-right corner, stacked top-to-bottom
ann_df <- auc_summary %>%
  mutate(
    x = 0.98,
    # space them out vertically (adjust spacing to taste)
    y = seq(0.15, by = -0.05, length.out = nrow(auc_summary)),
    label = paste("AUC =", round(AUC, 3))
  )

# 8) Plot all curves
p <- ggplot(
    roc_data,
    aes(
      x = fpr, 
      y = tpr, 
      color = Curve,
      group = Curve  # ensures ggplot doesn't use shapes for grouping
    )
  ) +
  # Use geom_line with key_glyph="path" to make sure the legend is drawn as lines
  geom_line(size = 1, key_glyph = "path") +
  # Diagonal line for no-discrimination
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "SVM model AUC ROC Plot",
    x = "False Positive Rate",
    y = "True Positive Rate",
    color = "Model"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  # Apply your custom color mapping, in the order of the factor levels
  scale_color_manual(values = color_vec) +
  # If a discrete shape scale is being auto-added, turn it off:
  scale_shape_discrete(guide = "none") +
  # Force the legend to display only colored lines (no shapes, no letters)
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(
    shape = NA,        # no shape
    linetype = 1,      # a regular line
    key_glyph = "path" # and definitely use a line glyph
  ))) +
  # Add AUC text in the bottom-right corner
  geom_text(
    data = ann_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = Curve),
    hjust = 1,  # right-align
    size = 4
  )

# 9) Save the plot
ggsave(
  filename = "PlotsAndPictures/Combined_AUCROCPlots/SVMPlot.png",
  plot = p,
  width = 8,
  height = 6
)

```

XGBoost Plot

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1) Trapezoidal rule for AUC
calc_auc <- function(x, y) {
  sum(diff(x) * (head(y, -1) + tail(y, -1)) / 2)
}

# 2) Define ROCData file paths (in any order) and their descriptive labels
roc_files <- c(
  "Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/ROCData_XGBoostNoFeatureEngineering.csv",
  "Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/ROCData_XGBoostNoFeatureEngineeringPCA.csv",
  "Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/ROCData_XGBoostWithFeatureEngineering.csv",
  "Base_Models_Data/XGBoost/ROCData_XGBoostWithFeatureEngineeringPCA.csv"
)

# We'll map the files to labels initially (the final legend order will follow AUC)
curve_labels <- c(
  "No pre-processing + No feature engineering",
  "No pre-processing + Feature engineering",
  "Pre-processing + No feature engineering",
  "Pre-processing + Feature engineering"
)

# 3) Read, compute AUC, store results
roc_list <- list()
auc_values <- numeric(length(roc_files))

for(i in seq_along(roc_files)) {
  df <- read_csv(roc_files[i], show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    arrange(fpr)  # ensure ascending fpr
  
  this_auc <- calc_auc(df$fpr, df$tpr)
  auc_values[i] <- this_auc
  
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(AUC = this_auc,
           Curve = curve_labels[i])
  
  roc_list[[i]] <- df
}

# 4) Combine all ROC data
roc_data <- bind_rows(roc_list)

# 5) Determine factor order by descending AUC
auc_summary <- roc_data %>%
  group_by(Curve) %>%
  summarize(AUC = unique(AUC), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(AUC))

# Convert "Curve" to a factor with levels in descending AUC order
roc_data$Curve <- factor(roc_data$Curve, levels = auc_summary$Curve, ordered = TRUE)

# 6) Define a custom color vector to match the descending AUC order.
#    Highest AUC -> green, second -> blue, third -> purple, fourth -> red
color_vec <- c("green", "blue", "purple", "red")

# 7) Build annotation data for bottom-right corner, stacked top-to-bottom
ann_df <- auc_summary %>%
  mutate(
    x = 0.98,
    # space them out vertically (adjust spacing to taste)
    y = seq(0.15, by = -0.05, length.out = nrow(auc_summary)),
    label = paste("AUC =", round(AUC, 3))
  )

# 8) Plot all curves
p <- ggplot(
    roc_data,
    aes(
      x = fpr, 
      y = tpr, 
      color = Curve,
      group = Curve  # ensures ggplot doesn't use shapes for grouping
    )
  ) +
  # Use geom_line with key_glyph="path" to make sure the legend is drawn as lines
  geom_line(size = 1, key_glyph = "path") +
  # Diagonal line for no-discrimination
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "XGBoost model AUC ROC Plot",
    x = "False Positive Rate",
    y = "True Positive Rate",
    color = "Model"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  # Apply your custom color mapping, in the order of the factor levels
  scale_color_manual(values = color_vec) +
  # If a discrete shape scale is being auto-added, turn it off:
  scale_shape_discrete(guide = "none") +
  # Force the legend to display only colored lines (no shapes, no letters)
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(
    shape = NA,        # no shape
    linetype = 1,      # a regular line
    key_glyph = "path" # and definitely use a line glyph
  ))) +
  # Add AUC text in the bottom-right corner
  geom_text(
    data = ann_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = Curve),
    hjust = 1,  # right-align
    size = 4
  )

# 9) Save the plot
ggsave(
  filename = "PlotsAndPictures/Combined_AUCROCPlots/XGBoostPlot.png",
  plot = p,
  width = 8,
  height = 6
)

```

Random Forest Plot

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1) Trapezoidal rule for AUC
calc_auc <- function(x, y) {
  sum(diff(x) * (head(y, -1) + tail(y, -1)) / 2)
}

# 2) Define ROCData file paths (in any order) and their descriptive labels
roc_files <- c(
  "Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ROCData_RandomForestNoFeatureEngineering.csv",
  "Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ROCData_RandomForestNoFeatureEngineeringPCA.csv",
  "Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ROCData_RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering.csv",
  "Base_Models_Data/Random_Forest/ROCData_RandomForestWithFeatureEngineeringPCA.csv"
)

# We'll map the files to labels initially (the final legend order will follow AUC)
curve_labels <- c(
  "No pre-processing + No feature engineering",
  "No pre-processing + Feature engineering",
  "Pre-processing + No feature engineering",
  "Pre-processing + Feature engineering"
)

# 3) Read, compute AUC, store results
roc_list <- list()
auc_values <- numeric(length(roc_files))

for(i in seq_along(roc_files)) {
  df <- read_csv(roc_files[i], show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    arrange(fpr)  # ensure ascending fpr
  
  this_auc <- calc_auc(df$fpr, df$tpr)
  auc_values[i] <- this_auc
  
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(AUC = this_auc,
           Curve = curve_labels[i])
  
  roc_list[[i]] <- df
}

# 4) Combine all ROC data
roc_data <- bind_rows(roc_list)

# 5) Determine factor order by descending AUC
auc_summary <- roc_data %>%
  group_by(Curve) %>%
  summarize(AUC = unique(AUC), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(AUC))

# Convert "Curve" to a factor with levels in descending AUC order
roc_data$Curve <- factor(roc_data$Curve, levels = auc_summary$Curve, ordered = TRUE)

# 6) Define a custom color vector to match the descending AUC order.
#    Highest AUC -> green, second -> blue, third -> purple, fourth -> red
color_vec <- c("green", "blue", "purple", "red")

# 7) Build annotation data for bottom-right corner, stacked top-to-bottom
ann_df <- auc_summary %>%
  mutate(
    x = 0.98,
    # space them out vertically (adjust spacing to taste)
    y = seq(0.15, by = -0.05, length.out = nrow(auc_summary)),
    label = paste("AUC =", round(AUC, 3))
  )

# 8) Plot all curves
p <- ggplot(
    roc_data,
    aes(
      x = fpr, 
      y = tpr, 
      color = Curve,
      group = Curve  # ensures ggplot doesn't use shapes for grouping
    )
  ) +
  # Use geom_line with key_glyph="path" to make sure the legend is drawn as lines
  geom_line(size = 1, key_glyph = "path") +
  # Diagonal line for no-discrimination
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Random Forest model AUC ROC Plot",
    x = "False Positive Rate",
    y = "True Positive Rate",
    color = "Model"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  # Apply your custom color mapping, in the order of the factor levels
  scale_color_manual(values = color_vec) +
  
  scale_shape_discrete(guide = "none") +
  # Force the legend to display only colored lines (no shapes, no letters)
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(
    shape = NA,        # no shape
    linetype = 1,      # a regular line
    key_glyph = "path" # and definitely use a line glyph
  ))) +
  # Add AUC text in the bottom-right corner
  geom_text(
    data = ann_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = Curve),
    hjust = 1,  # right-align
    size = 4
  )

# 9) Save the plot
ggsave(
  filename = "PlotsAndPictures/Combined_AUCROCPlots/RandomForestPlot.png",
  plot = p,
  width = 8,
  height = 6
)

```


### Stacking Classifier


Logistic Regression
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries from your previous work
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(caret)
library(ggcorrplot)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(kableExtra)
library(knitr)
library(purrr)
library(kernlab)
library(BiocManager)
library(readr)
library(doParallel)
library(EBImage)
library(pROC)

# Additional libraries for the current code block
library(xgboost)       # For XGBoost-related functionality
library(randomForest) 
trainNoFeatureEngineeringPCA <- read_csv("Prepped_Data/testNoFeatureEngineeringAblationPCA.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
testNoFeatureEngineeringPCA <- read_csv("Prepped_Data/testNoFeatureEngineeringStackingPCA.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
trainNoFeatureEngineering <- read_csv("Prepped_Data/testNoFeatureEngineeringAblation.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
testNoFeatureEngineering <- read_csv("Prepped_Data/testNoFeatureEngineeringStacking.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
trainWithFeatureEngineering <- read_csv("Prepped_Data/testWithFeatureEngineeringAblation.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
testWithFeatureEngineering <- read_csv("Prepped_Data/testWithFeatureEngineeringStacking.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

XGBoostNoFeatureEngineeringPCA <- readRDS('Base_Models/XGBoostNoFeatureEngineeringPCA.rds')
SVMNoFeatureEngineering <- readRDS('Base_Models/SVMModelNoFeatureEngineering.rds')
RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering <- readRDS('Base_Models/RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering.rds')
```


```{r}

# --- Generate Base Model Predictions ---
# For XGBoost and SVM, predictions are assumed to come out directly without extra data transformation.
preds_train_XGB <- predict(
  XGBoostNoFeatureEngineeringPCA,
  newdata = trainNoFeatureEngineeringPCA,
  type    = "prob"
)[, 2]

preds_test_XGB  <- predict(
  XGBoostNoFeatureEngineeringPCA,
  newdata = testNoFeatureEngineeringPCA,
  type    = "prob"
)[, 2]

preds_train_SVM <- predict(SVMNoFeatureEngineering, newdata = trainNoFeatureEngineering, type = "prob")[,2]
preds_test_SVM  <- predict(SVMNoFeatureEngineering, newdata = testNoFeatureEngineering, type = "prob")[,2]

# For the Random Forest model (using caret, with probability output)
preds_train_RF <- predict(RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering,
                          newdata = trainWithFeatureEngineering,
                          type = "prob")[,2]
preds_test_RF  <- predict(RandomForestWithFeatureEngineering,
                          newdata = testWithFeatureEngineering,
                          type = "prob")[,2]
```




```{r}
# --- Build Meta Training and Test Sets ---
meta_train <- data.frame(
  XGB   = preds_train_XGB,
  SVM   = preds_train_SVM,
  RF    = preds_train_RF,
  label = trainNoFeatureEngineeringPCA$label
)

meta_test <- data.frame(
  XGB   = preds_test_XGB,
  SVM   = preds_test_SVM,
  RF    = preds_test_RF,
  label = testNoFeatureEngineeringPCA$label

)

write.csv(meta_train, "Prepped_Data/StackedTrain.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(meta_test, "Prepped_Data/StackedTest.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```




logistic regression
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(caret)
library(ggcorrplot)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(kableExtra)
library(knitr)
library(purrr)
library(kernlab)
library(BiocManager)
library(readr)
library(doParallel)
library(EBImage)
library(pROC)
# (Optional) Shuffle
set.seed(100)
meta_train <- meta_train[sample(nrow(meta_train)), ]
# ensure it's a factor, but don't remap 0/1
if (!is.factor(meta_train$label)) {
  meta_train$label <- factor(meta_train$label)
}

train_control <- trainControl(
  method          = "cv",
  number          = 5,
  summaryFunction = defaultSummary,
  classProbs      = TRUE,
  savePredictions = "all"
)

# --- Train Meta Learner ---
set.seed(100)
meta_model <- train(
  label ~ .,
  data     = meta_train,
  method   = "glm",
  family   = binomial,
  metric   = "Accuracy",
  trControl= train_control
  #verbosity=0
)


saveRDS(meta_model, file = "Stacked_Models/LogReg.rds")
cat("Model saved as 'Stacked_Models/LogReg.rds'. To load it later, use:\n")
cat("loaded_model <- readRDS('Stacked_Models/LogReg.rds')\n\n")

###########################################
# 2. Save bestTune hyperparameters as CSV
###########################################
best_tune_df <- cbind(model = "LogReg", meta_model$bestTune)
write.csv(best_tune_df, "Stacked_Models_Data/LogReg/BestHyperparams_LogReg.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("BestTune hyperparameters saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/LogReg/BestHyperparams_LogReg.csv'.\n\n")

###########################################
# 3. Make predictions and create confusion matrix CSV with TP, FP, FN, TN
###########################################
# Predict on the test set (for class labels)
predictions <- predict(meta_model, newdata = meta_test)

# 1. Make sure your true labels are a factor, and capture their levels
meta_test$label <- factor(meta_test$label)
true_lvls <- levels(meta_test$label)
print(true_lvls)
# e.g. [1] "Natural" "Drowsy"

# 2. Inspect what your predictions look like
str(predictions)
# If it’s a character vector, coerce to factor first:
# predictions <- factor(predictions)

# 3. Now force your predictions to have exactly the same levels as the reference:
predictions <- factor(predictions, levels = true_lvls)
print(levels(predictions))
# Should be identical to true_lvls

# 4. If your model never predicted one of the classes, that factor level will now exist 
#    (with zero counts), which is fine.

# 5. Finally call confusionMatrix:
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(
  data      = predictions,
  reference = meta_test$label
)
print(conf_matrix)

levels_labels <- levels(meta_test$label)
if(length(levels_labels) == 2) {
  positive_class <- levels_labels[1]  # "Drowsy"
  negative_class <- levels_labels[2]  # "Natural"
  
  cm_table <- conf_matrix$table
  TP <- cm_table[positive_class, positive_class]
  FP <- cm_table[positive_class, negative_class]
  FN <- cm_table[negative_class, positive_class]
  TN <- cm_table[negative_class, negative_class]
  
  cm_df <- data.frame(model = "LogReg",
                      TP = TP,
                      FP = FP,
                      FN = FN,
                      TN = TN)
  
  write.csv(cm_df, "Stacked_Models_Data/LogReg/ConfusionMatrix_LogReg.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Confusion matrix saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/LogReg/ConfusionMatrix_LogReg.csv'.\n\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 4. Calculate and save F1-score, Precision, Recall, Accuracy, and AUC as CSV
  ###########################################
  accuracy <- conf_matrix$overall["Accuracy"]
  recall <- conf_matrix$byClass["Sensitivity"]
  precision <- conf_matrix$byClass["Pos Pred Value"]
  f1_score <- 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)
  
  # Obtain probability predictions for ROC curve (now that we enabled probabilities)
  probs <- predict(meta_model, newdata = meta_test, type = "prob")
  # Use probability for the positive class ("Drowsy")
  roc_obj <- roc(response = meta_test$label, predictor = probs[[positive_class]])
  auc_val <- as.numeric(auc(roc_obj))
  
  metrics_df <- data.frame(model = "LogReg",
                           Accuracy = accuracy,
                           Precision = precision,
                           Recall = recall,
                           FOne = f1_score,
                           AUC = auc_val)
  
  write.csv(metrics_df, "Stacked_Models_Data/LogReg/Metrics_LogReg.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Performance metrics saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/LogReg/Metrics_LogReg.csv'.\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 5. Create and save an AUC ROC plot as a PNG
  ###########################################
  # Build data for ROC curve: fpr (1 - specificity) and tpr (sensitivity)
  roc_data <- data.frame(fpr = 1 - roc_obj$specificities, tpr = roc_obj$sensitivities)
  
  write.csv(roc_data, "Stacked_Models_Data/LogReg/ROCData_LogReg.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("ROC Data for plot building saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/LogReg/ROCData_LogReg.csv'.\n")
  
  # Generate the ROC plot using ggplot2.
  roc_plot <- ggplot(roc_data, aes(x = fpr, y = tpr)) +
    geom_line(color = "blue") +  # ROC curve in blue
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +  # Dotted 45° reference line
    labs(x = "False Positive Rate (1 - Specificity)",
         y = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)",
         title = "ROC Curve for SVMWithFeatureEngineering") +
    theme_minimal() +
    # Annotate AUC in the top right corner, rounded to 3 significant figures.
    annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = 0.95, 
             label = paste("AUC =", format(round(auc_val, 3), nsmall = 3)), 
             color = "red", size = 5)
  
  # Save the ROC plot as a PNG file.
  ggsave("Stacked_Models_Data/LogReg/ROC_LogReg.png", 
         plot = roc_plot, width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)
  cat("ROC plot saved as 'Base_Models_Data/SVM/ROC_SVMWithFeatureEngineering.png'.\n")
  
} else {
  cat("Skipping confusion matrix extraction and ROC analysis: classification is not binary.\n")
}
```

```{r}
train_control <- trainControl(
  method          = "cv",
  number          = 5,
  summaryFunction = defaultSummary,
  classProbs      = TRUE,
  savePredictions = "all"
)

# --- Train Meta Learner ---
set.seed(100)
meta_model <- train(
  label ~ .,
  data     = meta_train,
  method   = "svmLinear",
  #family   = binomial,
  metric   = "Accuracy",
  trControl= train_control,
  verbosity=0
)


saveRDS(meta_model, file = "Stacked_Models/LinSVM.rds")
cat("Model saved as 'Stacked_Models/LinSVM.rds'. To load it later, use:\n")
cat("loaded_model <- readRDS('Stacked_Models/LinSVM.rds')\n\n")

###########################################
# 2. Save bestTune hyperparameters as CSV
###########################################
best_tune_df <- cbind(model = "LinSVM", meta_model$bestTune)
write.csv(best_tune_df, "Stacked_Models_Data/LinSVM/BestHyperparams_LinSVM.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("BestTune hyperparameters saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/LinSVM/BestHyperparams_LinSVM.csv'.\n\n")

###########################################
# 3. Make predictions and create confusion matrix CSV with TP, FP, FN, TN
###########################################
# Predict on the test set (for class labels)
predictions <- predict(meta_model, newdata = meta_test)

# 1. Make sure your true labels are a factor, and capture their levels
meta_test$label <- factor(meta_test$label)
true_lvls <- levels(meta_test$label)
print(true_lvls)
# e.g. [1] "Natural" "Drowsy"

# 2. Inspect what your predictions look like
str(predictions)
# If it’s a character vector, coerce to factor first:
# predictions <- factor(predictions)

# 3. Now force your predictions to have exactly the same levels as the reference:
predictions <- factor(predictions, levels = true_lvls)
print(levels(predictions))
# Should be identical to true_lvls

# 4. If your model never predicted one of the classes, that factor level will now exist 
#    (with zero counts), which is fine.

# 5. Finally call confusionMatrix:
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(
  data      = predictions,
  reference = meta_test$label
)
print(conf_matrix)

levels_labels <- levels(meta_test$label)
if(length(levels_labels) == 2) {
  positive_class <- levels_labels[1]  # "Drowsy"
  negative_class <- levels_labels[2]  # "Natural"
  
  cm_table <- conf_matrix$table
  TP <- cm_table[positive_class, positive_class]
  FP <- cm_table[positive_class, negative_class]
  FN <- cm_table[negative_class, positive_class]
  TN <- cm_table[negative_class, negative_class]
  
  cm_df <- data.frame(model = "LinSVM",
                      TP = TP,
                      FP = FP,
                      FN = FN,
                      TN = TN)
  
  write.csv(cm_df, "Stacked_Models_Data/LinSVM/ConfusionMatrix_LinSVM.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Confusion matrix saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/LinSVM/ConfusionMatrix_LinSVM.csv'.\n\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 4. Calculate and save F1-score, Precision, Recall, Accuracy, and AUC as CSV
  ###########################################
  accuracy <- conf_matrix$overall["Accuracy"]
  recall <- conf_matrix$byClass["Sensitivity"]
  precision <- conf_matrix$byClass["Pos Pred Value"]
  f1_score <- 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)
  
  # Obtain probability predictions for ROC curve (now that we enabled probabilities)
  probs <- predict(meta_model, newdata = meta_test, type = "prob")
  # Use probability for the positive class ("Drowsy")
  roc_obj <- roc(response = meta_test$label, predictor = probs[[positive_class]])
  auc_val <- as.numeric(auc(roc_obj))
  
  metrics_df <- data.frame(model = "LinSVM",
                           Accuracy = accuracy,
                           Precision = precision,
                           Recall = recall,
                           FOne = f1_score,
                           AUC = auc_val)
  
  write.csv(metrics_df, "Stacked_Models_Data/LinSVM/Metrics_LinSVM.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Performance metrics saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/LinSVM/Metrics_LinSVM.csv'.\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 5. Create and save an AUC ROC plot as a PNG
  ###########################################
  # Build data for ROC curve: fpr (1 - specificity) and tpr (sensitivity)
  roc_data <- data.frame(fpr = 1 - roc_obj$specificities, tpr = roc_obj$sensitivities)
  
  write.csv(roc_data, "Stacked_Models_Data/LinSVM/ROCData_LinSVM.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("ROC Data for plot building saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/LinSVM/ROCData_LinSVM.csv'.\n")
  
  # Generate the ROC plot using ggplot2.
  roc_plot <- ggplot(roc_data, aes(x = fpr, y = tpr)) +
    geom_line(color = "blue") +  # ROC curve in blue
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +  # Dotted 45° reference line
    labs(x = "False Positive Rate (1 - Specificity)",
         y = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)",
         title = "ROC Curve for SVMWithFeatureEngineering") +
    theme_minimal() +
    # Annotate AUC in the top right corner, rounded to 3 significant figures.
    annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = 0.95, 
             label = paste("AUC =", format(round(auc_val, 3), nsmall = 3)), 
             color = "red", size = 5)
  
  # Save the ROC plot as a PNG file.
  ggsave("Stacked_Models_Data/LinSVM/ROC_LinSVM.png", 
         plot = roc_plot, width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)
  cat("ROC plot saved as 'Base_Models_Data/SVM/ROC_SVMWithFeatureEngineering.png'.\n")
  
} else {
  cat("Skipping confusion matrix extraction and ROC analysis: classification is not binary.\n")
}
```

XGBoost Stacked

```{r}
train_control <- trainControl(
  method          = "cv",
  number          = 5,
  summaryFunction = defaultSummary,
  classProbs      = TRUE,
  savePredictions = "all"
)

# --- Train Meta Learner ---
set.seed(100)
meta_model <- train(
  label ~ .,
  data     = meta_train,
  method   = "xgbTree",
  #family   = binomial,
  metric   = "Accuracy",
  trControl= train_control,
  verbosity=0
)


saveRDS(meta_model, file = "Stacked_Models/XGBoost.rds")
cat("Model saved as 'Stacked_Models/XGBoost.rds'. To load it later, use:\n")
cat("loaded_model <- readRDS('Stacked_Models/XGBoost.rds')\n\n")

###########################################
# 2. Save bestTune hyperparameters as CSV
###########################################
best_tune_df <- cbind(model = "XGBoost", meta_model$bestTune)
write.csv(best_tune_df, "Stacked_Models_Data/XGBoost/BestHyperparams_XGBoost.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("BestTune hyperparameters saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/XGBoost/BestHyperparams_XGBoost.csv'.\n\n")

###########################################
# 3. Make predictions and create confusion matrix CSV with TP, FP, FN, TN
###########################################
# Predict on the test set (for class labels)
predictions <- predict(meta_model, newdata = meta_test)

# 1. Make sure your true labels are a factor, and capture their levels
meta_test$label <- factor(meta_test$label)
true_lvls <- levels(meta_test$label)
print(true_lvls)
# e.g. [1] "Natural" "Drowsy"

# 2. Inspect what your predictions look like
str(predictions)
# If it’s a character vector, coerce to factor first:
# predictions <- factor(predictions)

# 3. Now force your predictions to have exactly the same levels as the reference:
predictions <- factor(predictions, levels = true_lvls)
print(levels(predictions))
# Should be identical to true_lvls

# 4. If your model never predicted one of the classes, that factor level will now exist 
#    (with zero counts), which is fine.

# 5. Finally call confusionMatrix:
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(
  data      = predictions,
  reference = meta_test$label
)
print(conf_matrix)

levels_labels <- levels(meta_test$label)
if(length(levels_labels) == 2) {
  positive_class <- levels_labels[1]  # "Drowsy"
  negative_class <- levels_labels[2]  # "Natural"
  
  cm_table <- conf_matrix$table
  TP <- cm_table[positive_class, positive_class]
  FP <- cm_table[positive_class, negative_class]
  FN <- cm_table[negative_class, positive_class]
  TN <- cm_table[negative_class, negative_class]
  
  cm_df <- data.frame(model = "XGBoost",
                      TP = TP,
                      FP = FP,
                      FN = FN,
                      TN = TN)
  
  write.csv(cm_df, "Stacked_Models_Data/XGBoost/ConfusionMatrix_XGBoost.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Confusion matrix saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/XGBoost/ConfusionMatrix_XGBoost.csv'.\n\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 4. Calculate and save F1-score, Precision, Recall, Accuracy, and AUC as CSV
  ###########################################
  accuracy <- conf_matrix$overall["Accuracy"]
  recall <- conf_matrix$byClass["Sensitivity"]
  precision <- conf_matrix$byClass["Pos Pred Value"]
  f1_score <- 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)
  
  # Obtain probability predictions for ROC curve (now that we enabled probabilities)
  probs <- predict(meta_model, newdata = meta_test, type = "prob")
  # Use probability for the positive class ("Drowsy")
  roc_obj <- roc(response = meta_test$label, predictor = probs[[positive_class]])
  auc_val <- as.numeric(auc(roc_obj))
  
  metrics_df <- data.frame(model = "XGBoost",
                           Accuracy = accuracy,
                           Precision = precision,
                           Recall = recall,
                           FOne = f1_score,
                           AUC = auc_val)
  
  write.csv(metrics_df, "Stacked_Models_Data/XGBoost/Metrics_XGBoost.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Performance metrics saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/XGBoost/Metrics_XGBoost.csv'.\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 5. Create and save an AUC ROC plot as a PNG
  ###########################################
  # Build data for ROC curve: fpr (1 - specificity) and tpr (sensitivity)
  roc_data <- data.frame(fpr = 1 - roc_obj$specificities, tpr = roc_obj$sensitivities)
  
  write.csv(roc_data, "Stacked_Models_Data/XGBoost/ROCData_XGBoost.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("ROC Data for plot building saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/XGBoost/ROCData_XGBoost.csv'.\n")
  
  # Generate the ROC plot using ggplot2.
  roc_plot <- ggplot(roc_data, aes(x = fpr, y = tpr)) +
    geom_line(color = "blue") +  # ROC curve in blue
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +  # Dotted 45° reference line
    labs(x = "False Positive Rate (1 - Specificity)",
         y = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)",
         title = "ROC Curve for SVMWithFeatureEngineering") +
    theme_minimal() +
    # Annotate AUC in the top right corner, rounded to 3 significant figures.
    annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = 0.95, 
             label = paste("AUC =", format(round(auc_val, 3), nsmall = 3)), 
             color = "red", size = 5)
  
  # Save the ROC plot as a PNG file.
  ggsave("Stacked_Models_Data/XGBoost/ROC_XGBoost.png", 
         plot = roc_plot, width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)
  cat("ROC plot saved as 'Base_Models_Data/SVM/ROC_SVMWithFeatureEngineering.png'.\n")
  
} else {
  cat("Skipping confusion matrix extraction and ROC analysis: classification is not binary.\n")
}
```

Random Forest Stacked
```{r}
train_control <- trainControl(
  method          = "cv",
  number          = 5,
  summaryFunction = defaultSummary,
  classProbs      = TRUE,
  savePredictions = "all"
)

# --- Train Meta Learner ---
set.seed(100)
meta_model <- train(
  label ~ .,
  data     = meta_train,
  method   = "rf",
  #family   = binomial,
  metric   = "Accuracy",
  trControl= train_control,
  verbosity=0
)


saveRDS(meta_model, file = "Stacked_Models/RandomForest.rds")
cat("Model saved as 'Stacked_Models/RandomForest.rds'. To load it later, use:\n")
cat("loaded_model <- readRDS('Stacked_Models/RandomForest.rds')\n\n")

###########################################
# 2. Save bestTune hyperparameters as CSV
###########################################
best_tune_df <- cbind(model = "RandomForest", meta_model$bestTune)
write.csv(best_tune_df, "Stacked_Models_Data/RandomForest/BestHyperparams_RandomForest.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("BestTune hyperparameters saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/RandomForest/BestHyperparams_RandomForest.csv'.\n\n")

###########################################
# 3. Make predictions and create confusion matrix CSV with TP, FP, FN, TN
###########################################
# Predict on the test set (for class labels)
predictions <- predict(meta_model, newdata = meta_test)

# 1. Make sure your true labels are a factor, and capture their levels
meta_test$label <- factor(meta_test$label)
true_lvls <- levels(meta_test$label)
print(true_lvls)
# e.g. [1] "Natural" "Drowsy"

# 2. Inspect what your predictions look like
str(predictions)
# If it’s a character vector, coerce to factor first:
# predictions <- factor(predictions)

# 3. Now force your predictions to have exactly the same levels as the reference:
predictions <- factor(predictions, levels = true_lvls)
print(levels(predictions))
# Should be identical to true_lvls

# 4. If your model never predicted one of the classes, that factor level will now exist 
#    (with zero counts), which is fine.

# 5. Finally call confusionMatrix:
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(
  data      = predictions,
  reference = meta_test$label
)
print(conf_matrix)

levels_labels <- levels(meta_test$label)
if(length(levels_labels) == 2) {
  positive_class <- levels_labels[1]  # "Drowsy"
  negative_class <- levels_labels[2]  # "Natural"
  
  cm_table <- conf_matrix$table
  TP <- cm_table[positive_class, positive_class]
  FP <- cm_table[positive_class, negative_class]
  FN <- cm_table[negative_class, positive_class]
  TN <- cm_table[negative_class, negative_class]
  
  cm_df <- data.frame(model = "RandomForest",
                      TP = TP,
                      FP = FP,
                      FN = FN,
                      TN = TN)
  
  write.csv(cm_df, "Stacked_Models_Data/RandomForest/ConfusionMatrix_RandomForest.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Confusion matrix saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/RandomForest/ConfusionMatrix_RandomForest.csv'.\n\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 4. Calculate and save F1-score, Precision, Recall, Accuracy, and AUC as CSV
  ###########################################
  accuracy <- conf_matrix$overall["Accuracy"]
  recall <- conf_matrix$byClass["Sensitivity"]
  precision <- conf_matrix$byClass["Pos Pred Value"]
  f1_score <- 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)
  
  # Obtain probability predictions for ROC curve (now that we enabled probabilities)
  probs <- predict(meta_model, newdata = meta_test, type = "prob")
  # Use probability for the positive class ("Drowsy")
  roc_obj <- roc(response = meta_test$label, predictor = probs[[positive_class]])
  auc_val <- as.numeric(auc(roc_obj))
  
  metrics_df <- data.frame(model = "RandomForest",
                           Accuracy = accuracy,
                           Precision = precision,
                           Recall = recall,
                           FOne = f1_score,
                           AUC = auc_val)
  
  write.csv(metrics_df, "Stacked_Models_Data/RandomForest/Metrics_RandomForest.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Performance metrics saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/RandomForest/Metrics_RandomForest.csv'.\n")
  
  ###########################################
  # 5. Create and save an AUC ROC plot as a PNG
  ###########################################
  # Build data for ROC curve: fpr (1 - specificity) and tpr (sensitivity)
  roc_data <- data.frame(fpr = 1 - roc_obj$specificities, tpr = roc_obj$sensitivities)
  
  write.csv(roc_data, "Stacked_Models_Data/RandomForest/ROCData_RandomForest.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  cat("ROC Data for plot building saved as 'Stacked_Models_Data/RandomForest/ROCData_RandomForest.csv'.\n")
  
  # Generate the ROC plot using ggplot2.
  roc_plot <- ggplot(roc_data, aes(x = fpr, y = tpr)) +
    geom_line(color = "blue") +  # ROC curve in blue
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +  # Dotted 45° reference line
    labs(x = "False Positive Rate (1 - Specificity)",
         y = "True Positive Rate (Sensitivity)",
         title = "ROC Curve for SVMWithFeatureEngineering") +
    theme_minimal() +
    # Annotate AUC in the top right corner, rounded to 3 significant figures.
    annotate("text", x = 0.75, y = 0.95, 
             label = paste("AUC =", format(round(auc_val, 3), nsmall = 3)), 
             color = "red", size = 5)
  
  # Save the ROC plot as a PNG file.
  ggsave("Stacked_Models_Data/RandomForest/ROC_RandomForest.png", 
         plot = roc_plot, width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)
  cat("ROC plot saved as 'Base_Models_Data/SVM/ROC_SVMWithFeatureEngineering.png'.\n")
  
} else {
  cat("Skipping confusion matrix extraction and ROC analysis: classification is not binary.\n")
}
```

### Final Plot Constructions

```{R}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Define the base path
base_path <- "Stacked_Models_Data/LogReg/"

# List files, excluding ROCData and ROC files (only reading Metrics, ConfusionMatrix, and BestHyperparams)
metrics_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^Metrics_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
confusion_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^ConfusionMatrix_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
hyperparam_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^BestHyperparams_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

# Function to extract model label from filename given a specific prefix.
# For example, for filename "Metrics_LogRegNoFeatureEngineeringPCA.csv" with prefix "Metrics_",
# the function will return "LogRegNoFeatureEngineeringPCA".
extract_model_label <- function(filename, prefix) {
  label <- basename(filename)
  label <- sub(paste0("^", prefix), "", label)
  label <- sub("\\.csv$", "", label)
  return(label)
}

# Create a named vector of confusion files keyed by their extracted model label.
confusion_map <- setNames(confusion_files, sapply(confusion_files, extract_model_label, prefix = "ConfusionMatrix_"))

# Initialize a list to store the joined classification data frames
classification_list <- list()

# Loop over each Metrics file and find the corresponding ConfusionMatrix file by an exact filename match.
for (metrics_file in metrics_files) {
  # Extract the model label from the Metrics file name.
  m_model <- extract_model_label(metrics_file, prefix = "Metrics_")
  
  # Look up the corresponding ConfusionMatrix file using the full model label.
  if (m_model %in% names(confusion_map)) {
    conf_file <- confusion_map[[m_model]]
    
    # Read the Metrics CSV and ConfusionMatrix CSV. They already include a "model" column.
    metrics_df <- read_csv(metrics_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
    confusion_df <- read_csv(conf_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
    
    # Join the two data frames by the "model" column.
    joined_df <- inner_join(metrics_df, confusion_df, by = "model")
    
    # Store the joined data frame in a list.
    classification_list[[m_model]] <- joined_df
  } else {
    warning("No matching ConfusionMatrix file found for model '", m_model, "'.")
  }
}

# Rbind (stack) all joined classification reports into one data frame
combined_classification <- bind_rows(classification_list)

# Read and rbind all BestHyperparams CSV files
hyperparams_list <- lapply(hyperparam_files, function(file) {
  read_csv(file, show_col_types = FALSE)
})
combined_hyperparameters <- bind_rows(hyperparams_list)

# Write the combined data frames to CSV files
write_csv(combined_classification, file.path(base_path, "LogRegCombinedClassificationReport.csv"))
write_csv(combined_hyperparameters, file.path(base_path, "LogRegHyperparameters.csv"))

# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Define the base path
base_path <- "Stacked_Models_Data/LinSVM/"

# List files, excluding ROCData and ROC files (only reading Metrics, ConfusionMatrix, and BestHyperparams)
metrics_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^Metrics_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
confusion_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^ConfusionMatrix_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
hyperparam_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^BestHyperparams_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

# Function to extract model label from filename given a specific prefix.
# For example, for filename "Metrics_LinSVMNoFeatureEngineeringPCA.csv" with prefix "Metrics_",
# the function will return "LinSVMNoFeatureEngineeringPCA".
extract_model_label <- function(filename, prefix) {
  label <- basename(filename)
  label <- sub(paste0("^", prefix), "", label)
  label <- sub("\\.csv$", "", label)
  return(label)
}

# Create a named vector of confusion files keyed by their extracted model label.
confusion_map <- setNames(confusion_files, sapply(confusion_files, extract_model_label, prefix = "ConfusionMatrix_"))

# Initialize a list to store the joined classification data frames
classification_list <- list()

# Loop over each Metrics file and find the corresponding ConfusionMatrix file by an exact filename match.
for (metrics_file in metrics_files) {
  # Extract the model label from the Metrics file name.
  m_model <- extract_model_label(metrics_file, prefix = "Metrics_")
  
  # Look up the corresponding ConfusionMatrix file using the full model label.
  if (m_model %in% names(confusion_map)) {
    conf_file <- confusion_map[[m_model]]
    
    # Read the Metrics CSV and ConfusionMatrix CSV. They already include a "model" column.
    metrics_df <- read_csv(metrics_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
    confusion_df <- read_csv(conf_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
    
    # Join the two data frames by the "model" column.
    joined_df <- inner_join(metrics_df, confusion_df, by = "model")
    
    # Store the joined data frame in a list.
    classification_list[[m_model]] <- joined_df
  } else {
    warning("No matching ConfusionMatrix file found for model '", m_model, "'.")
  }
}

# Rbind (stack) all joined classification reports into one data frame
combined_classification <- bind_rows(classification_list)

# Read and rbind all BestHyperparams CSV files
hyperparams_list <- lapply(hyperparam_files, function(file) {
  read_csv(file, show_col_types = FALSE)
})
combined_hyperparameters <- bind_rows(hyperparams_list)

# Write the combined data frames to CSV files
write_csv(combined_classification, file.path(base_path, "LinSVMCombinedClassificationReport.csv"))
write_csv(combined_hyperparameters, file.path(base_path, "LinSVMHyperparameters.csv"))

# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Define the base path
base_path <- "Stacked_Models_Data/XGBoost/"

# List files, excluding ROCData and ROC files (only reading Metrics, ConfusionMatrix, and BestHyperparams)
metrics_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^Metrics_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
confusion_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^ConfusionMatrix_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
hyperparam_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^BestHyperparams_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

# Function to extract model label from filename given a specific prefix.
```
