```{R}
# For example, for filename "Metrics_XGBoostNoFeatureEngineeringPCA.csv" with prefix "Metrics_",
# the function will return "XGBoostNoFeatureEngineeringPCA".
extract_model_label <- function(filename, prefix) {
  label <- basename(filename)
  label <- sub(paste0("^", prefix), "", label)
  label <- sub("\\.csv$", "", label)
  return(label)
}

# Create a named vector of confusion files keyed by their extracted model label.
confusion_map <- setNames(confusion_files, sapply(confusion_files, extract_model_label, prefix = "ConfusionMatrix_"))

# Initialize a list to store the joined classification data frames
classification_list <- list()

# Loop over each Metrics file and find the corresponding ConfusionMatrix file by an exact filename match.
for (metrics_file in metrics_files) {
  # Extract the model label from the Metrics file name.
  m_model <- extract_model_label(metrics_file, prefix = "Metrics_")
  
  # Look up the corresponding ConfusionMatrix file using the full model label.
  if (m_model %in% names(confusion_map)) {
    conf_file <- confusion_map[[m_model]]
    
    # Read the Metrics CSV and ConfusionMatrix CSV. They already include a "model" column.
    metrics_df <- read_csv(metrics_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
    confusion_df <- read_csv(conf_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
    
    # Join the two data frames by the "model" column.
    joined_df <- inner_join(metrics_df, confusion_df, by = "model")
    
    # Store the joined data frame in a list.
    classification_list[[m_model]] <- joined_df
  } else {
    warning("No matching ConfusionMatrix file found for model '", m_model, "'.")
  }
}

# Rbind (stack) all joined classification reports into one data frame
combined_classification <- bind_rows(classification_list)

# Read and rbind all BestHyperparams CSV files
hyperparams_list <- lapply(hyperparam_files, function(file) {
  read_csv(file, show_col_types = FALSE)
})
combined_hyperparameters <- bind_rows(hyperparams_list)

# Write the combined data frames to CSV files
write_csv(combined_classification, file.path(base_path, "XGBoostCombinedClassificationReport.csv"))
write_csv(combined_hyperparameters, file.path(base_path, "XGBoostHyperparameters.csv"))

# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Define the base path
base_path <- "Stacked_Models_Data/RandomForest/"

# List files, excluding ROCData and ROC files (only reading Metrics, ConfusionMatrix, and BestHyperparams)
metrics_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^Metrics_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
confusion_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^ConfusionMatrix_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
hyperparam_files <- list.files(path = base_path, pattern = "^BestHyperparams_.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

# Function to extract model label from filename given a specific prefix.
# For example, for filename "Metrics_RandomForestNoFeatureEngineeringPCA.csv" with prefix "Metrics_",
# the function will return "RandomForestNoFeatureEngineeringPCA".
extract_model_label <- function(filename, prefix) {
  label <- basename(filename)
  label <- sub(paste0("^", prefix), "", label)
  label <- sub("\\.csv$", "", label)
  return(label)
}

# Create a named vector of confusion files keyed by their extracted model label.
confusion_map <- setNames(confusion_files, sapply(confusion_files, extract_model_label, prefix = "ConfusionMatrix_"))

# Initialize a list to store the joined classification data frames
classification_list <- list()

# Loop over each Metrics file and find the corresponding ConfusionMatrix file by an exact filename match.
for (metrics_file in metrics_files) {
  # Extract the model label from the Metrics file name.
  m_model <- extract_model_label(metrics_file, prefix = "Metrics_")
  
  # Look up the corresponding ConfusionMatrix file using the full model label.
  if (m_model %in% names(confusion_map)) {
    conf_file <- confusion_map[[m_model]]
    
    # Read the Metrics CSV and ConfusionMatrix CSV. They already include a "model" column.
    metrics_df <- read_csv(metrics_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
    confusion_df <- read_csv(conf_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
    
    # Join the two data frames by the "model" column.
    joined_df <- inner_join(metrics_df, confusion_df, by = "model")
    
    # Store the joined data frame in a list.
    classification_list[[m_model]] <- joined_df
  } else {
    warning("No matching ConfusionMatrix file found for model '", m_model, "'.")
  }
}

# Rbind (stack) all joined classification reports into one data frame
combined_classification <- bind_rows(classification_list)

# Read and rbind all BestHyperparams CSV files
hyperparams_list <- lapply(hyperparam_files, function(file) {
  read_csv(file, show_col_types = FALSE)
})
combined_hyperparameters <- bind_rows(hyperparams_list)

# Write the combined data frames to CSV files
write_csv(combined_classification, file.path(base_path, "RandomForestCombinedClassificationReport.csv"))
write_csv(combined_hyperparameters, file.path(base_path, "RandomForestHyperparameters.csv"))

```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Define file paths for each combined classification report
rf_report <- "Stacked_Models_Data/RandomForest/RandomForestCombinedClassificationReport.csv"
xgb_report <- "Stacked_Models_Data/XGBoost/XGBoostCombinedClassificationReport.csv"
svm_report <- "Stacked_Models_Data/LinSVM/LinSVMCombinedClassificationReport.csv"
logreg_report <- "Stacked_Models_Data/LogReg/LogRegCombinedClassificationReport.csv"

# Read each CSV into a data frame
rf_df <- read_csv(rf_report, show_col_types = FALSE)
xgb_df <- read_csv(xgb_report, show_col_types = FALSE)
svm_df <- read_csv(svm_report, show_col_types = FALSE)
lr_df <- read_csv(logreg_report, show_col_types = FALSE)


# Rbind (stack) the three data frames into one and then sort by accuracy then ROC (both in descending order)
final_report <- bind_rows(rf_df, xgb_df, svm_df,lr_df) %>%
  arrange(desc(Accuracy), desc(AUC))

# Define the output path and write the final combined data frame as CSV
output_path <- "Stacked_Models_Data/FinalStackedModelReport.csv"
write_csv(final_report, output_path)
```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# Define file paths for each combined classification report
rf_report <- "Stacked_Models_Data/RandomForest/RandomForestHyperparameters.csv"
xgb_report <- "Stacked_Models_Data/XGBoost/XGBoostHyperparameters.csv"
svm_report <- "Stacked_Models_Data/LinSVM/LinSVMHyperparameters.csv"
lr_report <- "Stacked_Models_Data/LogReg/LogRegHyperparameters.csv"

# Read each CSV into a data frame
rf_df <- read_csv(rf_report, show_col_types = FALSE)
xgb_df <- read_csv(xgb_report, show_col_types = FALSE)
svm_df <- read_csv(svm_report, show_col_types = FALSE)


# Define the output path and write the final combined data frame as CSV
write_csv(rf_df, "Stacked_Models_Data/RandomForestHyperparameters.csv")
write_csv(xgb_df, "Stacked_Models_Data/XGBoostHyperparameters.csv")
write_csv(svm_df, "Stacked_Models_Data/SVMHyperparameters.csv")
```


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1) Trapezoidal rule for AUC
calc_auc <- function(x, y) {
  sum(diff(x) * (head(y, -1) + tail(y, -1)) / 2)
}

# 2) Define ROCData file paths (in any order) and their descriptive labels
roc_files <- c(
  "Stacked_Models_Data/XGBoost/ROCData_XGBoost.csv",
  "Stacked_Models_Data/LinSVM/ROCData_LinSVM.csv",
  "Stacked_Models_Data/LogReg/ROCData_LogReg.csv",
  "Stacked_Models_Data/RandomForest/ROCData_RandomForest.csv"
)

# We'll map the files to labels initially (the final legend order will follow AUC)
curve_labels <- c(
  "Stacked XGBoost",
  "Stacked Linear SVM",
  "Stacked Logistic Regression",
  "Stacked Random Forest"
)

# 3) Read, compute AUC, store results
roc_list <- list()
auc_values <- numeric(length(roc_files))

for(i in seq_along(roc_files)) {
  df <- read_csv(roc_files[i], show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    arrange(fpr)  # ensure ascending fpr
  
  this_auc <- calc_auc(df$fpr, df$tpr)
  auc_values[i] <- this_auc
  
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(AUC = this_auc,
           Curve = curve_labels[i])
  
  roc_list[[i]] <- df
}

# 4) Combine all ROC data
roc_data <- bind_rows(roc_list)

# 5) Determine factor order by descending AUC
auc_summary <- roc_data %>%
  group_by(Curve) %>%
  summarize(AUC = unique(AUC), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(AUC))

# Convert "Curve" to a factor with levels in descending AUC order
roc_data$Curve <- factor(roc_data$Curve, levels = auc_summary$Curve, ordered = TRUE)

# 6) Define a custom color vector to match the descending AUC order.
#    Highest AUC -> green, second -> blue, third -> purple, fourth -> red
color_vec <- c("green", "blue", "purple", "red")

# 7) Build annotation data for bottom-right corner, stacked top-to-bottom
ann_df <- auc_summary %>%
  mutate(
    x = 0.98,
    # space them out vertically (adjust spacing to taste)
    y = seq(0.15, by = -0.05, length.out = nrow(auc_summary)),
    label = paste("AUC =", round(AUC, 3))
  )

# 8) Plot all curves
p <- ggplot(
  roc_data,
  aes(
    x = fpr, 
    y = tpr, 
    color = Curve,
    group = Curve  # ensures ggplot doesn't use shapes for grouping
  )
) +
  # Use geom_line with key_glyph="path" to make sure the legend is drawn as lines
  geom_line(size = 1, key_glyph = "path") +
  # Diagonal line for no-discrimination
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Stacked model AUC ROC Plot",
    x = "False Positive Rate",
    y = "True Positive Rate",
    color = "Model"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  # Apply your custom color mapping, in the order of the factor levels
  scale_color_manual(values = color_vec) +
  # If a discrete shape scale is being auto-added, turn it off:
  scale_shape_discrete(guide = "none") +
  # Force the legend to display only colored lines (no shapes, no letters)
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(
    shape = NA,        # no shape
    linetype = 1,      # a regular line
    key_glyph = "path" # and definitely use a line glyph
  ))) +
  # Add AUC text in the bottom-right corner
  geom_text(
    data = ann_df,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = label, color = Curve),
    hjust = 1,  # right-align
    size = 4
  )

# 9) Save the plot
ggsave(
  filename = "PlotsAndPictures/Combined_AUCROCPlots/StackedPlot.png",
  plot = p,
  width = 8,
  height = 6
)

```

# Code Appendix

```{r codeAppend, ref.label=knitr::all_labels(), echo=TRUE, eval=FALSE}
```
